reasoning: A is the strongest reasoning artifact: it explicitly catches the substitution trap in (b) (P(STEM) unconditional vs P(STEM | STEM-or-business), and that the third bucket means it is not a two-way split), separates epistemic from input uncertainty, runs both city/metro readings, and ties its tightest 80% range (24-36%) to a stricter undergraduate-only definition it names as the load-bearing source of uncertainty. B is the most empirically grounded, citing Crist|Kolder 2025 (29% eng + 7% science) and BLS OEWS numbers, which is a real strength, but it leans on those figures with less scrutiny of definitional risk and surfaces fewer cognitive traps; its 34%/28-40% sits on a mixed F500/S&P sample it only lightly haircuts. C reasons cleanly with two independent paths in (a) but its BLS cross-check is loose (5,000-8,000 national, then arbitrary halving), it offers no concrete data for (b), and its STEM central (~33%) drifts upward without anchoring the way A does. A wins on trap-awareness and calibration discipline; B edges C on evidence quality.
